{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_85","type":"agent_message","text":"ARCHITECT DECISIONS:
- Dispatch std nonblocking owner-loop experiment: post-scaffold oracle is green, profile remains ~0.56x hotspot median, and current code still pays per-client thread/DB mutex costs.
- Use transitional DB storage: `runtime-owner-4` must use existing `global_databases()` handles, not a divergent live `Vec<RedisDb>`.
- Preserve socket ownership: plain-TCP owner loop drains per-slot foreign-payload receivers; no foreign thread writes owner-loop sockets.
- Keep long-term gates: real poller dependency, TLS migration, I/O threads, and soak runner stay human-gated.

FILES UPDATED:
- `docs/RUNTIME_OWNER_OVERNIGHT_ARCHITECTURE.md`: added current evidence and the `runtime-owner-4` implementation contract.
- `harness/architecture/decisions/runtime-ownership.md`: locked the std owner-loop contract and clarified long-term TODOs.
- `harness/architecture/lifecycle-map.toml`: marked std experiment approved and added invariants.
- `harness/work-packets.jsonl`: strengthened `runtime-owner-4-std-nonblocking-owner-loop`.
- `harness/completion.toml`: updated target/non-goals and latest evidence pointers.

PACKETS CREATED OR CHANGED:
- `runtime-owner-4-std-nonblocking-owner-loop`: perf-fixer, plain TCP std nonblocking owner loop, depends on `runtime-owner-3-overnight-architecture`.

HUMAN QUESTIONS:
- Long-term only: production poller choice, TLS migration sequencing, I/O thread parity, and soak runner scope. None block `runtime-owner-4`.

NEXT SAFE DISPATCH:
- `runtime-owner-4-std-nonblocking-owner-loop`"}}
